title: poa sterilis subsp. versicolor
intro: Poa sterilis subsp. versicolor, also known as fescue grass, is a subspecies of the grass family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Poa sterilis subsp. versicolor can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
